author: y4rr
content:
Spun out of #583. The hook surface lives on \`HookedModule<M>\` (the wrapper struct in \`ferrotorch-nn/src/hooks.rs\`) — concrete users wrap their module to opt into hooks. That works but it isn't quite torch parity, where every \`nn.Module\` has \`register_forward_hook\` / \`register_forward_pre_hook\` / \`register_full_backward_hook\` directly.

## Why it wasn't done in #583
Putting \`register_forward_hook(&self, ...)\` on the trait requires every \`Module\` impl to own a hook registry. That's:
1. A field every concrete module must have (or a default registry that lives elsewhere).
2. Interior mutability (Mutex / RwLock) on each module since registration happens through \`&self\` after construction.
3. Hooks need to fire during \`forward\` — concrete \`forward\` impls would need to call into the registry, which means the trait method \`forward\` either becomes a wrapper or every impl gets boilerplate.

## Proposed shape
Option A — embed registry in trait: add \`fn hooks(&self) -> Option<&HookRegistry<T>>\` returning None by default. Provide a default \`forward_with_hooks(&self, input)\` method that:
1. runs registered pre-hooks → modified input
2. calls \`self.forward(input)\`
3. runs registered post-hooks (input, output)
4. returns output

Modules that opt in store a \`HookRegistry<T>\` field and override \`hooks()\`. Callers use \`forward_with_hooks\` instead of \`forward\` when they want hooks.

Option B — derive macro \`#[derive(Module)]\` that auto-generates the registry field + impl. Lower friction for users, more upfront work to land.

Option C — keep \`HookedModule\` as the canonical opt-in (status quo). Add convenience constructors on the wrapper.

Backward hooks need autograd integration (attaching to the output tensor's grad_fn), which is a separate piece of work — current \`HookedModule::register_backward_hook\` stores the hook but never fires it. That's the bigger gap.
